chief_complaint: 43-year-old South Asian man with acute chest pain and diagnosed with an anterior ST-elevation myocardial infarction.
history: Newly diagnosed type 2 diabetes, elevated cholesterol, primary percutaneous coronary intervention with drug-eluting stent placement, two additional episodes of acute in-stent thrombosis within 17 days, repeat balloon angioplasty, second stent, and administration of intravenous antiplatelet agents.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: ECG: anterior ST-elevation myocardial infarction, Bloods: elevated cholesterol, Imaging: angiography, optical coherence tomography, Left ventricular ejection fraction: ~30% at day 16, 40-45% by month 6.
